cit.basis: Original Estonian model (Income Tax Act / Tulumaksuseadus): no annual CIT on retained or reinvested profit; tax is due only upon profit distribution at 22/78 of the NET distribution from 1 January 2025 (raised from 20/80; the preferential 14/86 regular-dividend rate was abolished as of 2025).
cit.gross_up: Rate is expressed on the net amount: net 78 -> tax 22 -> gross 100, i.e. 22% of the gross distribution and approximately 28.2% effective on the net distribution.
cit.note_2026: Security/defence levy package: the distribution rate becomes 24/76 of the net distribution from 1 January 2026 (note only, not modelled for 2025).
cit.pillar_two_note: Estonia applies the EU Minimum Tax Directive (Pillar Two) transitional deferral for jurisdictions with few in-scope groups; large multinational groups (>= EUR 750m revenue) may face top-up tax elsewhere in the group despite the 0% rate on retained profit.

vat.note: Käibemaks (VAT): standard rate 22% in H1 2025, RAISED to 24% from 1 July 2025 under the Security Tax Act; EU One-Stop-Shop applies.

personal_income_tax.note: Flat personal income tax of 22% from 2025 (raised from 20%).

social_tax.note: Sotsiaalmaks: 33% employer social tax (20% pension, 13% health insurance); plus unemployment insurance contributions (employer 0.8%, employee 1.6%) and funded-pension withholding.

effective_date_range.basis: Target ruleset/params year; jurisdiction fiscal calendars and transitional periods may differ.

rate_threshold_assumptions: Rates and thresholds are simplified for LedgerField estimates.
Deemed distributions, fringe benefits, non-business expenses, WHT, relief, filing, and sector-specific rules may alter outcomes.
Verify against official guidance and local advisor before filing or production use.
limitations: Not legal, tax, accounting, or filing advice.
Does not encode every deduction, exemption, treaty, WHT, payroll, or filing rule.
High-stakes use requires local validation against official forms and current law.
